<!-- GENERATED by scripts/generate.ts from the public @bloom/sdk operation-manifest. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Bloom MCP tool catalog

13 public tools, grouped by what they are for. Every tool is `experimental` — expect the surface to move. Full input/output schemas live in each tool's own JSON schema (exposed by the MCP server); this file is the map, not the schema.

## auth

Confirm who you are and what you can reach.

### `whoami`

Returns { userId, narrowingKind, accessibleProjectCount }. Sanity check for 'is my token working / what can it do.'

## discovery

Orient: find the project, branch, and canvas pages to work in.

### `list_projects`

List accessible projects. Returns { projects: [{ projectId, name, workspaceId, workspaceName }] }.

### `list_branches`

List branches on a project. { projectId } → { branches: [{ branchId, name, isMain, status }] }.

### `list_canvas_pages`

List canvas pages for a branch's project. REQUIRED for multi-page projects — the page resolver errors with MCP_PAGE_AMBIGUOUS when a branch has >1 pages and no explicit canvasPageId.

## canvas

Read and write **canvas frames** — React TSX at `@bloom-frame/<uuid>`, not host-repo files.

### `write_files`

Create or overwrite Bloom CANVAS frames. Paths are `@bloom-frame/<uuid>` (or `@bloom-frame/_new_<localName>` placeholders that get minted to UUIDs; the result echoes `placeholderMintingMap`). Each entry: { path, content, kind?: 'artboard'|'canvasComponent', basis? } OR mockup { path, kind: 'mockup', mockup: { routePath, deviceConfig? }, basis? }. `kind: 'canvasComponent'` on create makes the frame importable from other frames; on overwrite, kind is ignored — use `edit_files` to promote later. NOT FOR HOST-REPO FILES: `@/<path>` resolves to a Bloom project's sandbox, NOT the repo your editor has open. Pass packageVersions when the app package graph is unavailable. Sandbox-path entries are rejected (MCP_SANDBOX_PATH_REJECTED). Scope: canvas:write.

### `edit_files`

Edit existing Bloom CANVAS frames (paths `@bloom-frame/<uuid>`). Per entry, either a source-only Morph fast-apply (instructions + code_edit) OR a kind change (artboard → canvasComponent promotion; demote not supported). The two are mutually exclusive. Pass packageVersions when the app package graph is unavailable. Sandbox paths rejected. Scope: canvas:write.

### `read_files`

Read canonical TSX source of Bloom CANVAS frames (`@bloom-frame/<uuid>`) or read-only Bloom sandbox repo files (`apps/default/app/index.tsx`). Sandbox reads use Bloom's project sandbox, not the repo your editor has open. Direct sandbox writes are not available via MCP; use `trigger_agent` for full-stack changes. Scope: project:read.

### `delete_files`

Delete Bloom CANVAS frames (paths `@bloom-frame/<uuid>`). Idempotent on missing. Errors with DELETE_FRAME_HAS_IMPORTERS if other frames still import this one — edit / delete the importers first, or wrap them via edit_files. Scope: canvas:write.

### `code_search`

Search Bloom CANVAS frames (`target: 'canvas'`) or the Bloom sandbox codebase (`target: 'codebase'`). Canvas search is branch-chain aware and indexed. Codebase search uses the same WarpGrep/sandbox path as the Bloom agent. `target: 'public_repo'` is intentionally not exposed; use the MCP client's native repo/browser search. Scope: project:read.

### `copy_files`

Copy a Bloom sandbox file onto the canvas as an import wrapper. This does NOT copy implementation source into a frame; it creates a canvas artboard that imports the sandbox file (`mode: 'import_wrapper'`) and materializes the codebase-component bridge. Supported v1 direction: sandbox → canvas. Scope: canvas:write.

## agent

Hand off full-stack / codebase work to the Bloom agent on a branch.

### `trigger_agent`

Ask the Bloom agent to perform full-stack/codebase work on a branch. This starts a normal chat-turn edit and uses the existing agent sync/deploy lifecycle. Args are intentionally small: { branchId?, prompt }. Use this instead of direct MCP sandbox writes. Scope: project:write.

## comments

Leave and read review comments anchored to frames.

### `post_comment`

Post a comment anchored to a Bloom canvas frame (creates a new thread). Use to report what you changed, or to flag a specific frame for the user's review. Reply-to-existing-thread and resolve are not in Phase 0 — every call opens a new thread. Scope: comments:write.

### `list_comments`

List comments scoped to a branch (optionally filtered by frameId). Use to read user feedback before responding. Returns up to `limit` (default 100). Scope: project:read.
